reasoning:
ACLED data shows a baseline of 0.000 events for the months prior to October 2025. However, following the contested October 12, 2025 Presidential election and the October 27 official results, widespread violence erupted in multiple cities. ACLED's own November 2025 Africa Overview explicitly describes elements of these events as "rioters [who] barricaded roads," nearly guaranteeing they will be coded as "Riots", which will push the 360-day baseline average before Nov 23, 2025, significantly above 0. 

For Nov 30 (lookback Oct 31-Nov 29): This period captures the tail end of the post-election protests, which media reports indicate "continued through November 1," as well as Paul Biya's swearing-in on November 6. Because the 360-day average dilutes the total number of riots over 12 months, the 30-day window before Nov 30 only needs to contain more than ~8.3% of the total riots. Given the spread of events over late Oct and early Nov, this threshold is likely to be met (probability > 0.5).

For Dec 23 (lookback Nov 23-Dec 22): The government responded with lethal force (48+ deaths reported) and mass arrests, and opposition leaders shifted tactics to non-violent "ghost town" lockdowns by Nov 3-5. Riots are highly likely to have died out entirely by late November/December, falling below the newly inflated baseline. 

For Feb 21 (lookback Jan 22-Feb 20): Riots are similarly expected to be at or near 0, failing to beat the baseline, though uncertainty inevitably increases as we look three months out into the future. My probabilities reflect a return to the long-term low baseline of riots, with appropriately increasing uncertainty.
